title: Skill pipeline meta-layer initiation
description: Stand up the canonical infrastructure for the LuhTech skill pipeline: skill.schema.json, Python generator, JSON-canonical / markdown-generated pattern, CI workflows, directory structure, test fixture. The deliverable that enables all subsequent skill instance authoring across the holdings.

scope.scopeLock: Branch is scope-locked to the files above. Any file outside this list requires a SPIKE or scope amendment. No CI debt fixes, no rule instance authoring, no other schema work in-scope here.

outputs.name: skill.schema.json v1.0.0
outputs.description: Canonical schema for skill instances. Draft 7. Six categories enum, four-state lifecycleStatus, three-state triggerMode, lenient pairedRule (optional), evidenceClass required on UP-promotions to formalized (if/then conditional), structured triggerContext + behavior, generationMetadata for CI drift detection.
acceptanceCriteria: File validates as Draft 7 (Draft7Validator.check_schema passes)
Required fields: $schema, $id, name, description, version, category, lifecycleStatus, triggerMode, triggerContext, behavior, authority, transitions (12 total)
additionalProperties: false at top level
$id pattern enforces urn:luhtech:skill:<category>:<name>:<semver>
category enum matches the six confirmed: org, dev-pipeline, comms, governance, infra, venture
if/then conditional on transitions: to=formalized requires evidenceClass

outputs.name: Python generator (skill_generate.py)
outputs.description: Deterministic JSON-to-SKILL.md transform. Reads skill-instance.json, validates against skill.schema.json, generates YAML frontmatter + markdown body, stamps generationMetadata (generator version, generatedAt, sourceJsonSha). Refuses on schema validation failure. Output is byte-stable across re-runs given identical input.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI: python3 tools/skill_generate.py <path-to-skill-instance.json>
Output written to sibling SKILL.md path
Validates input against skill.schema.json before generating; refuses on failure with non-zero exit
Generated SKILL.md begins with YAML frontmatter (---) containing name, description, version, category
Markdown body sections: Trigger, Behavior, Examples (if present), Boundaries (if present), References (if present)
Footer comment block: <!-- generated by luhtech-skill-generate v<version> from <sourceJsonSha> at <ISO timestamp> -->
Re-running the generator on unchanged input produces byte-identical output (SHA-stable)
Refuses to run if Python jsonschema package not available

outputs.name: Python validator (skill_validate.py)
outputs.description: Validates a skill-instance.json against skill.schema.json. Returns 0 on pass, non-zero on fail with line-cited errors. Wrapper script used by CI workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI: python3 tools/skill_validate.py <path-to-skill-instance.json>
Exits 0 on valid instance
Exits non-zero on invalid instance with structured error output (field path + error message)
Validates the test fixture instance and exits 0

outputs.name: skill-instance-validation.yml CI workflow
outputs.description: GitHub Actions workflow that runs skill_validate.py against every skill-instance.json under skills/_instances/. Triggered on pull_request when paths skills/_instances/** or schemas/skill.schema.json change. Required check on PRs touching skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow file is valid YAML
Triggers on pull_request with path filter for skills/_instances/** and schemas/skill.schema.json
Installs Python + jsonschema
Runs skill_validate.py against every skill-instance.json found under skills/_instances/
Fails the check if any instance fails validation
Runs cleanly on the test fixture and reports pass

outputs.name: skill-md-generation-check.yml CI workflow
outputs.description: GitHub Actions workflow that regenerates SKILL.md from skill-instance.json for every skill and diffs against the committed SKILL.md. Fails if drift detected. Enforces the JSON-canonical / MD-generated invariant mechanically.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow file is valid YAML
Triggers on pull_request with path filter for skills/_instances/**
Runs skill_generate.py on every skill-instance.json
Diffs the regenerated SKILL.md against the committed file
Fails the check if any drift detected, with diff output in the log
Passes cleanly on the test fixture

outputs.name: Directory structure for skill instances
outputs.description: Six category subdirectories created with .gitkeep files: org, dev-pipeline, comms, governance, infra, venture. Plus _test-fixture/ for the bootstrap test instance.
acceptanceCriteria: All six category directories exist: skills/_instances/{org,dev-pipeline,comms,governance,infra,venture}/
Each contains a .gitkeep file (so git tracks the empty dir)
skills/_instances/_test-fixture/fixture-skill/ contains both skill-instance.json and SKILL.md

outputs.name: .refsignore update
outputs.description: Add skills/_instances/** to .refsignore so refs-check.yml does not attempt to index skill instances as schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: .refsignore contains a line matching 'skills/_instances/**' (or equivalent glob)
refs-check.yml passes on this branch (no false positives on skill instances)

outputs.name: Test fixture skill instance
outputs.description: Minimal valid skill instance used to prove the generator + validator + CI work end-to-end. Includes skill-instance.json (canonical JSON) and SKILL.md (generator output). Not an active behavioral skill — purely a CI fixture.
acceptanceCriteria: skill-instance.json validates against skill.schema.json
SKILL.md is the exact output of running skill_generate.py on skill-instance.json
Re-running the generator produces byte-identical SKILL.md
skill-instance-validation.yml workflow passes on this fixture
skill-md-generation-check.yml workflow passes on this fixture

outputs.name: Deliverable instance self-record
outputs.description: This deliverable's own instance, recorded in the canonical deliverables registry. Self-referential: the deliverable that initiates the skill pipeline also records itself per existing deliverable.schema.json governance.
acceptanceCriteria: File validates against deliverable.schema.json
lifecycleState transitions S4-authored → S6-staged → S8-closed as the PR progresses
Final state captures commitSha and prUrl

validation.command: python3 -c "import json; from jsonschema import Draft7Validator; Draft7Validator.check_schema(json.load(open('schemas/skill.schema.json')))"

validation.expectedResult: Generator exits 0, diff produces no output (byte-identical)
Green — no skill instances misindexed as schemas
Green — fixture SKILL.md matches generator output

validation.step: All other existing required checks pass

retention.rationale: Schema and CI workflows are canonical infrastructure. Test fixture remains as the bootstrap proof; deletable only when superseded by a real working-model skill instance under a category.

transitions.trigger: Spec authored in SR-chat for L4 review

notes: This deliverable establishes the substrate. The first behavioral skill instances (session-open-research, substrate-before-write, speak-direct) ship in a follow-on deliverable D-SKILLS-FOUNDATION-1 after this lands.
Lenient pairing per Q3: pairedRule is optional. Skills can exist without paired rule instances.
Generator is single-direction: JSON → MD. Hand-edits to MD do not survive CI per OUT-5.
Test fixture is purely a CI bootstrap. It exercises the schema + generator + validator + workflows but is not a behavioral skill Claude should load.
